name: Tofu Jerky
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon Garlic powder or 1 clove crushed fresh garlic
1 lb Firm or extra firm tofu
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut and drain the tofu. Take a 1 lb block, cut it in half, and slice into strips on its short side. Strips should be about 4-5 mm thick; they will shrink to about half their size during cooking.
2. Mix all marinade ingredients well. Place tofu in a single layer in a shallow baking pan or cookie sheet and pour the marinade over it. Let soak for several hours or overnight.
3. Drain excess liquid (reuse!) and dry tofu using a food dehydrator, warm oven set to 200°F, or by placing it on a tray in an oven preheated to 200°F, flipping hourly for even drying. This may take 4-8 hours, depending on conditions. In sunny, dry climates, sun-drying is possible and takes all day.
4. The jerky is delicious and keeps indefinitely. Dry until very chewy but not crispy.
5. Experiment with variations: use low-sodium soy for less salt, Tabasco or ground cayenne for heat, chili powder for a spicy flavor, oregano and basil for a pizza-inspired taste.
